name: Salem (Oregon, United States)

colors.symbolism: the Willamette River — represented by the hoist stripe
the cherry blossom — Salem as "the Cherry City"

description: A white field with a blue vertical stripe on the hoist; in the centre-fly, a pink cherry blossom whose five petals enclose a small white five-pointed star.
history: Adopted on 11 July 2022 after a flag-redesign contest won by Jordan Keagle. The cherry blossom recalls Salem's nickname as "the Cherry City" (for its cherry trees); the white five-pointed star represents Salem's status as the capital of Oregon; the blue hoist stripe represents the Willamette River. It replaced the city's earlier 1972 flag.

features.symbolism: white field bearing the cherry blossom
the blue stripe on the hoist representing the Willamette River, on which Salem stands
the five-petalled cherry blossom recalls Salem's nickname "The Cherry City", for the cherry trees once central to its agricultural economy

features.role: white star at the centre of the cherry blossom
features.symbolism: the white five-pointed star at the centre of the blossom represents Salem's status as the capital of Oregon
